title: Cells, Walls, Passages, Boundaries, and Invariants · Language Clinic: Topology Validation
summary: One cell opens east while its neighbor stays closed west, boundary walls disappear, and rendering data becomes the maze source of truth. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the topology validation increment in reciprocal wall and passage model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reconcile cell count, neighbor pairs, reciprocal openings, boundary exceptions, and illegal diagonal links.
Keep maze topology independent from Canvas item identifiers, colors, widths, and animation state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 6544 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_wall_cell_invariants_3(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-topology-validation-1kvzhm
# Competency: maze-topology-validation.
# Case 3050: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: all graph claims need an independent witness.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_topology_validation_1kvzhm(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1kvzhm = "1kvzhm-mhzvk1-3050"
    assert evidence_variant_1kvzhm
    evidence_axes_1kvzhm = (
        "grid1kvzhm",
        "topology3050",
        "searchmhzvk1",
        "event1kvzhm3050",
        "access30501kvzhm",
        "recoverymhzvk13050",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1kvzhm)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reconcile cell count, neighbor pairs, reciprocal openings, boundary exceptions, and illegal diagonal links.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Topology Validation model · Theory Topology Validation, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Topology Validation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Topology Validation model · Theory Topology Validation, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2210 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; all graph claims need an independent witness; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Reconcile cell count, neighbor pairs, reciprocal openings, boundary exceptions, and illegal diagonal links.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Topology Validation model · Theory Topology Validation, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A maze that renders symmetrically has valid topology.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-topology-validation-1kvzhm.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-topology-validation-1kvzhm(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_topology_validation_1kvzhm\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reciprocal_openings != passage_edges)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?boundary_breaches > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?illegal_diagonals > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using reconcile and cell plus specific case evidence.
